comments.author: @Dan-i1i4n
comments.text: Kiev is the ancient capital of Russia and is the 2nd most important city in the entire Russian history, after Moscow. Kiev means more to Russia than New York means to the US, Berlin means to Germany, or Manchester means to England. For more than 350 years ancient Russia was governed from Kiev and all Russian elites (political/economic/religious) used to live there and govern from there, and only the Golden Horde in the mid-13th century was able to temporarily occupy Kiev, and partially destroy it and partially burn it out. Surviving Russian elites and most of the population had to flee from there to other Russian cities (Vladimir, etc.) and hide in the forests…….However, in 1686, reemerging Russia has bought back the Kiev’s ruins from Poland/the Commonwealth and then was able to rebuild it again from scratch.

comments.author: @vladkudrya9615
comments.text: Just a historical note: Kyiv has never surrendered. Whether it was the Mongols, the USSR, or the Germans, Kyiv always fought, even knowing it would lose. Kyiv lost battles, was repeatedly destroyed and occupied, but never surrendered without a fight. Why the Russians in 2022 decided that Kyiv would surrender upon seeing Russian troops is a mystery to me.

comments.author: @JR-mx5pn
comments.text: Russian state narratives have often portrayed Kievan Rus’ as the common origin of Russians, Ukrainians, and Belarusians. Ukrainian historians argue that modern Russia has appropriated Ukrainian historical heritage, while Russian historians often argue for a shared historical legacy. Russia simply took Ukrainian history and claimed it as their own. But with this war Russian state finally severed ties with Ukraine exposing their own inferiority. Ukraine will never forget this war and will be enemy to them forever.

comments.author: @JR-mx5pn
comments.text: Mongolian tribes that overran Russian territories for centuries, later intermixed with nomadic Slavic tribes, forming what is claimed to be today’s indigenous population of Russia. As a result,  Russians are said to have inherited Mongolian DNA and a propensity for destruction. According to this view, they lacked a great history of their own and instead began appropriating the history of neighboring nations. They allegedly claimed Ukrainian history as their own, and now seek to seize Ukrainian land while attempting to destroy people who are ethnically different from themselves.

replies.author: @SilhouetteSE
replies.text:
​​@hirdbarding3399   ​I am quite aware of those similarities. However, the pattern inside and around the dome is not Christian, it's Islamic. 
As someone born and raised in Russia, I've been to lots of Russian and Ukrainian Orthodox churches. But never in my life have I seen an Orthodox dome with a pattern like that. Normally, an Orthodox church will have an image of the Savior surrounded by a host of angels painted in the middle of the dome, and a series of tall narrow windows right below the drum.

replies.author: @CyberOdesa
replies.text: В англійській мові немає точного відповідника українського звука «и» у слові «Київ», тому англомовні люди замінюють його на звичний для них звук «і» й вимовляють Kyiv майже як Kiev. Не вимагайте неможливого.

replies.author: @CyberOdesa
replies.text:
@WhereonEarth4K The spelling itself is correct, but the pronunciation is wrong—it sounds like the Russian pronunciation, “Kiev.” Kyiv is pronounced approximately “KIH-yiv,” with the stress on the first syllable.
There is no exact equivalent of this sound in English. To pronounce it, start with the “i” in “bit,” then pull your tongue slightly farther back without rounding your lips.

comments.author: @gannakarnaukh7194
comments.text:
Ukraine is one of the most traumatized lands in Europe, and the scale of violence here is systematically underestimated. The mass shooting at Babi Yar was not the only tragedy or mass murder of Ukrainians!

NKVD executions in Sandarmokh, Bykivnia, Kurapaty, and Vinnytsia.
Network of mass graves:
•  In Bykivnia — over 100,000 victims
•  In Kurapaty — from 30,000 to 250,000
•  In Sandarmokh — about 9,000
•  In Vinnytsia — over 9,500

The genocide of Ukrainians in 1937–1938 (the NKVD’s “Polish Operation”) — This was one of the largest ethnic cleansings in 20th-century Europe.

•  111,091 Poles,
•  over 20,000 Ukrainians,
•  thousands of Belarusians and Lithuanians.

Executions in the prisons of Western Ukraine (June 1941). As the NKVD retreated before the advancing Germans, they massacred prisoners in Lviv, Dubno, Lutsk, Ternopil, and Stanislaviv (Ivano-Frankivsk). The death toll: between 20,000 and 40,000 victims in just a few days.

Not to mention the GULAG as a system for the destruction of Ukrainians. According to various estimates, more than 1 million Ukrainians passed through the GULAG — the flower of the nation. Hundreds of thousands perished.

replies.author: @gannakarnaukh7194
replies.text:
@u@user-kk9zf8lq8o Yes, it was horrible. 😢My family also suffered greatly! In reality, there were 3 Holodomors in Ukraine:

The first Holodomor (1921–1923) — in southern Ukraine. The communists seized grain from Ukrainians in the same way.

The second Holodomor (1932–1933) — the largest and most tragic one.

The third Holodomor (1946–1947) — which happened after WW II. It was the same fascist policy of the Kremlin: seizing food for export.

replies.author: @user-kk9zf8lq8o
replies.text: You forgot about the most terrible of all crimes - the artificial famine, the genocide of the Ukrainian people of 1932-1933. In which Soviet inhumans mercilessly starved more than 7 million!!! Ukrainians. Including many of my relatives - my great-grandmother, two children of my grandmother, many others. More than half of my grandparents' village in Vinnytsia region was killed by a terrible famine. The goal was to destroy Ukrainians and our culture, to take away people's land and property, to make resistance impossible, to sow fear for decades. And the inhumans succeeded.

comments.author: @AlexanderReef
comments.text: Thank you for your documentaries about Ukraine. They convey its spirit, even though some things are not perfect here or there. For example, at the beginning of this one: "Kyiv is older than Moscow by 400 years". No, Kyiv is much older. Moscow was founded in 1147, Kyiv in (approx.) 482. That makes it 665 years older.

replies.author: @gannakarnaukh7194
replies.text:
Actually the name "Moskau" was first mentioned in 1147, and it referred to the river, not a settlement.

A small settlement with the Finno-Ugric name "Moskva" was founded only in 1272 by Khan Mengu-Timur. It was not the village of Moscow, but the Vladimir-Suzdal Principality that was under the rule of the Golden Horde, paid tribute to the khans, and was part of the Tatar administrative system.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: The Novgorod dating question is one of those moments where the chronicles become genuinely unreliable — thank you for that. 🌿

replies.author: @Anton_Danylchenko
replies.text: @Chaldon-hl6yk тризуб использовался только начиная с Владимира (у Святослава был двозуб). Єто конец X века. Все что было до Святослава - легендарные события, ничем не подтвержденные кроме Повести Временных Лет. Сама легенда про Рюрика может оказаться всего лише легендой. Тризуб на гербе Украины - чтобы показать преемственность Украины от Руси Владимира и Ярослава (от централизованого государства ядром которого был Киев). Все что было до єтого - не более чем викингские набеги варягов из Киева  и из других мест на соседние славянские племена и не имеет ничего общего с государственностью.

replies.author: @Anton_Danylchenko
replies.text: It is not necessary to be a professor to confirm this. There is literally no archealogical confirmation of any fofrtified settlement on the place of Novgorod before the X century (before 930). The only source that claims existence of Novgorod in IX century is Primary Chronicle or Tale of Bygone Years, which was written in XII century and which credibility for events before the middle of X century is very questionable. Basically anithing before the reign of Olga and Sviatoslav is legendary and has no proof in other sources (even the events during the reign of Olga is partially legendary). The centralised Rus state was basically create in the end of X century during the reign of Volodymyr. Everything before those days is about some Viking tribe that made raids from Kyiv on different Slavic tribes and not about the state called Rus.

replies.author: @Anton_Danylchenko
replies.text: This is highly disputed. With absolute certainty there was a fortified settlement on the main hill in IX century. However, its history before the middle of X century is mostly legendary and based only on a single source - Primary Chronicle or Tale of Bygone Years  - which was written in XII century and which credibility for events before the middle of X century is questionable. There indeed could be a settlement here before IX century - but it has not been proven yet archaeologically and if such settlment existed it could be non-fortified and it is yet to be proven whether it could be called Kyiv or not. "1500" years is an invented date.

replies.author: @tetyanas4026
replies.text:
@Alexandr-ss5hyв вашем утверждении сразу две манипуляции: не на древнерусском, а церковнославянском; не Киев, а Кыєвь. 
Древнерусский существовал только на на территории, которую вы называете северо-восточная Русь, т е Заонсье, т е современные российские территории. На территории современной Украины люди разговаривали на другом языке: староукраинском. Все летописи писались на церковнославянском с элементами древнекиевского диалекта. А написание Киев - современное русское.
